steps: Data set A has 9 dots with the middle (5th) dot at 7, so the median of A is 7. Data set B also has 9 dots with its middle dot at 7, so the median of B is 7. Statement I (equal medians) is true.

Data set A spans values 4 through 10 (range 6). Data set B spans values 5 through 9 (range 4). A wider spread means a larger standard deviation, so A's standard deviation exceeds B's. Statement II (equal standard deviations) is false.
Only Statement I must be true.
choiceElimination:
• Choice A — 'I only': Statement I (equal medians) is true; Statement II (equal SDs) is false because A has greater spread. ✓
  • Choice B — 'II only': Statement II is false since A's range (6) exceeds B's range (4), giving A a larger SD. ✗
  • Choice C — 'I and II': Statement II is false, so both cannot be true. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Neither I nor II': Statement I is true (both medians = 7), so this is incorrect. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
